baseline_difficulty.evidence: Issue #649 provides complete acceptance criteria with specific node kinds (function_declaration, class_declaration, object_declaration, companion object, delegation_specifiers) and exact tree-sitter grammar version (0.3.8). Issues #607-609 (C#, C++, JS) show no additional codebase research questions arose beyond the issue text.

recent_language_prs: #609: feat(lang): add C# grammar support (595 additions)
#608: feat(lang): add C and C++ grammar support (373 additions)
#607: feat(lang): add JavaScript grammar support (242 additions)
prior_art.reasoning: All three recent add-language PRs follow an identical repeatable pattern: (1) add tree-sitter crate to root Cargo.toml workspace.dependencies; (2) add lang-LANG feature flag in code-analyze-core/Cargo.toml and to default feature set; (3) create languages/LANG.rs with 4-5 query constants and 1-2 handler functions; (4) register in mod.rs get_language_info() and get_ts_language() match arms; (5) register extension in lang.rs EXTENSION_MAP and supported_languages(); (6) write 8-12 unit tests using parse_LANG() helper. PR #609 shows 595 additions but only 392 lines are in new kotlin.rs file; 203 lines are test file additions. Each prior PR required ~250-400 lines of unique synthesis but the structure is identical. A lazy SCOUT following this pattern yields high scores; differentiation is limited.

synthesis_requirements.question: Does tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 export a public LANGUAGE constant and is it compatible with the current tree-sitter 0.26.6 crate ABI? (Prior test: verify export path and version string.)
synthesis_requirements.why_not_retrievable: Issue text states version 0.3.8 but does not confirm the actual public API or ABI compatibility. crates.io metadata shows 0.3.8 was released Aug 2024; tree-sitter 0.26.6 may have introduced breaking changes. Only the crate source or cargo build confirms this.
synthesis_requirements.answer_source: crates.io API + Cargo.lock + attempted cargo test --features lang-kotlin

synthesis_requirements.question: What are the exact node kinds for Kotlin companion objects and object declarations in tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8? The issue says extract object_declaration but does not specify whether a companion object inside a class is a separate node or part of class_declaration's children.
synthesis_requirements.why_not_retrievable: Issue text omits the actual tree-sitter node structure. tree-sitter-kotlin grammar must be consulted. A naive SCOUT guesses companion object is a separate declaration; this could be wrong, requiring a grammar spec or test-driven discovery.
synthesis_requirements.answer_source: /fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin grammar corpus or crate source code

synthesis_requirements.question: Does the Kotlin DEFUSE_QUERY requirement exist? Issue #649 mentions 7 acceptance criteria but does not mention defuse_query. However, recent C# PR #609 includes DEFUSE_QUERY as a feature. Is lang-kotlin expected to support def-use or not?
synthesis_requirements.why_not_retrievable: Issue #649 does not mention defuse_query. However, examining crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/mod.rs and recent PR #659 (def-use infrastructure) shows DEFUSE_QUERY is now a required field for new languages. The issue predates this requirement or was not updated. Only reading codebase and PR history clarifies whether DEFUSE_QUERY is now mandatory.

kotlin_gotchas.crate_status: tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 from crates.io, published 2024-08-03, maintained by fwcd (High reputation, Benchmark 79.1). Crate exists and is stable.
kotlin_gotchas.abi_compat: tree-sitter 0.26.6 is current in aptu-coder. No breaking ABI changes documented between tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 release (Aug 2024) and now. However, unverified without cargo build attempt. Prior removal in commit bc3c399 used tree-sitter-kotlin-ng 1.1.0 (different crate name); issue proposes fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin (different source). ABI risk is LOW but must be verified.
alternatives: tree-sitter-kotlin-ng (1.1.0) from crates.io: older, now unused in aptu-coder after commit bc3c399; fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin (0.3.8) is the recommended replacement per issue text
No official Kotlin grammar from tree-sitter org; fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin is community standard
kotlin_gotchas.kts_support: Issue text mentions .kts (script files) in acceptance criteria but does not specify tree-sitter-kotlin grammar support. Context7 docs show Kotlin class/function declarations but do not explicitly confirm .kts parsing. Only grammar inspection or test confirms whether .kts is a separate file_type node or same as .kt parsing.

recommended_rubric_criteria.criterion: tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 public LANGUAGE export verified and ABI-compatible with tree-sitter 0.26.6
Kotlin node kinds documented and distinguish companion objects from object declarations in delegation_specifiers correctly
At least 3 query patterns from tree-sitter-kotlin corpus (function_declaration, class_declaration, object_declaration variants) correctly captured in ELEMENT_QUERY
extract_inheritance handler correctly walks delegation_specifiers and separates superclass_type_with_constructor (has parens) from user_type (no parens)
Unit tests confirm .kt AND .kts file parsing both work; at least 1 test with .kts syntax (e.g. top-level function, extension function)
DEFUSE_QUERY constant created for Kotlin if required by current LanguageInfo struct, or justified as None if not applicable (e.g. read issue context or PR #659)
All acceptance criteria from issue #649 met: feature flag added to default, all query constants present, extension registry updated, module registered, all tests pass

final_recommendation: SUITABLE_WITH_RUBRIC_DESIGN: Issue #649 provides clear acceptance criteria but exhibits high ceiling risk due to repeatable prior-art pattern (C#, C++, JS PRs). Rubric must include 6 synthesis criteria (C1-C6) requiring code-driven discovery and tree-sitter grammar verification, not just issue text retrieval. C1-C5 force real synthesis; C7 is trivial verification. This raises difficulty above Exp 2 baseline and prevents 100% saturation if any synthesis criterion is non-obvious.
